#  Add Photos to a Drawing from the Camera (iOS) 
##  Things to Consider 
  * **Required User Permissions:**
    * _To add photos to a drawing,_ 'Standard' permissions on the project's Drawings tool AND 'Read Only' or higher permissions on the project's Photos tool.  
_Note:_ Users with 'Read Only' permissions to the Photos tool can only select photos that already exist in the Photos tool, while users with 'Standard' or 'Admin' permissions can also add new photos.
    * _To publish personal markups to the Published layer_ , 'Standard' or 'Admin' on the project's Drawings tool.   
See [What is the difference between personal and published markups?](https://v2.support.procore.com/faq-what-is-the-difference-between-personal-and-published-drawing-markups)
  * **Additional Information:**
    * Photos pinned to a drawing _via the camera_ as described in this article do not show up in the 'Photos from Drawings' album. Photos added to drawings _from_ the Drawing tool will appear in the 'Photos from Drawings' album in the Photos tool.


##  Steps 
  1. Open the **Procore** app on your iOS mobile device.
  2. Do one of the following:
     * Tap the **Camera** tool.
     * Tap the **Photos** tool. Tap the **new** ![Icon Plus Quick Create Mobile2](https://images.ctfassets.net/plvbd9i6qkpm/a27be202634839c76584fe365cc247fb/1c03472023e1b0974c6f7b58a4235d9d/icon-plus-quick-create-mobile2.png?fm=webp&h=17) icon, then select **Camera**.
  3. Tap the drawing ![Icon Quick Capture Drawing Area Ios](https://images.ctfassets.net/plvbd9i6qkpm/57327f7123254b37d662540e0d470ab4/990371308ee1abf97561fee7ab7e386c/icon-quick-capture-drawing-area-ios.png?fm=webp&h=24) icon to select a location on a drawing where you want the photo pinned.
  4. Drop a pin by tapping the area of the drawing where you want the photo linked after you take it.
  5. Tap **Done**.
  6. _Optional:_ Tap **Album** if you want the photo(s) saved to a different album in the Photos tool instead of the 'Unclassified' album.
  7. Take the photo.
     * Take a photo without mark up.
       * Tap the **shutter** to take a photo.
     * Take a photo with mark up.
       * Tap **Markup** to take the photo and immediately mark it up.
       * Tap the **shutter** to take a photo.
       * [Mark up the photo](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/photos-ios/tutorials/mark-up-a-photo-ios).
       * Do one of the following:
         * If you want to save both the original and marked up photo, mark the **Save as New Photo** option, then tap **Save.**
         * If you want to only save your marked up photo, clear the **Save as New Photo** option, then tap **Overwrite.**
  8. _Optional:_ Move or remove the pin and any linked photos by tapping on the drawing ![Icon Quick Capture Drawing Area Ios](https://images.ctfassets.net/plvbd9i6qkpm/57327f7123254b37d662540e0d470ab4/990371308ee1abf97561fee7ab7e386c/icon-quick-capture-drawing-area-ios.png?fm=webp&h=24) icon again from the camera tool.  
_Note:_ Moving the pin causes _all_ photos taken with the pin in the previous location to relocate to the pin's new location on the drawing.
  9. Tap **Done**.
  10. To take more photos but link them to a new location on the same drawing or a different drawing, exit the camera using the **x** icon and then open it again using the bottom camera ![Icon Camera](https://images.ctfassets.net/plvbd9i6qkpm/8cfa1e5add6c64e1f1d534bd618f1dd4/a6d59c8f144bcc8d27e4086533d42bc1/icon-camera.png?fm=webp&h=18) icon.


## See Also
  * [Take and Add Photos to an Album (iOS)](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/photos-ios/tutorials/take-and-add-photos-to-an-album)
  * [Create Items from Photos (iOS)](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/photos-ios/tutorials/create-items-from-photos)
title: Add Photos to a Drawing from the Camera (iOS)
description: How to link photos to a drawing from Procore's camera tool on an iOS mobile device.
